title: Kinfire Delve: Scorn's Stockade (review)
body:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Tb4JB3eFPXw

Daniel (@dungeondive) ventures into the punishing depths of Kinfire Delve: Scorn's Stockade, a compact card game that packs a brutal challenge into an impressively affordable package. At around $25, this Kevin Wilson design delivers component quality that punches well above its weight class—gorgeous cards with gold leaf embossing, sturdy custom dice, and a box so well-constructed that Daniel waxes almost poetic about it.

The gameplay involves matching suits to overcome challenges as you descend deeper into Scorn's hellish well, with each card presenting obstacles, traps, puzzles, and combat encounters that interact in deviously interconnected ways. Daniel admits he's been thoroughly humbled by this one—he's never come close to winning and suspects he never will. The game demands careful analysis of card interactions and optimal play decisions, making it potentially paralysis-inducing for those prone to overthinking.

With two heroes included and compatibility with other Kinfire Delve sets for expanded play, this small box game travels well, sets up quickly, and looks absolutely stunning on the table. Just be prepared for it to beat you senseless while doing so.

----

*Have you managed to conquer Scorn's Stockade, or does it remain an unconquered tormentor in your collection?*
